title: cerasus leveilleana f. intermedia
intro: Cerasus leveilleana f. intermedia, also known as the Sand Cherry or Korean Sand Cherry, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). Traditionally, the plant's roots, stems, and flowers have been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cerasus leveilleana f. intermedia are thought to include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and reduce symptoms of fever and cough. Herbal preparations of the plant may include teas, infusions, or decoctions made from its roots, stems, and flowers, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
